title: eryngium glomeratum subsp. glomeratum
intro: Eryngium glomeratum subsp. glomeratum, also known as the cluster sea holly, is a perennial flowering plant native to Europe, North Africa, and Western Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. The health benefits of this plant include relief from respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and improve digestive health. Eryngium glomeratum subsp. glomeratum can be prepared as a tea or tincture, and its flowers and roots can be used in herbal remedies to harness its medicinal properties.
